content:
- Marked the bug task `<status>completed</status>` with `<passes>true</passes>` and checked the acceptance criteria after all required lanes passed.
- Ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, which updated the Ralph task ledgers for the next task selection.
- Next I am staging all changes, committing with the required task-finished message including the green lane evidence, and pushing the branch.

- Read prior progress, refreshed Ralph task summaries, and applied the choose-task rules against the active backlog.
- Confirmed there are no active failing bug tasks; the only active failing work is story-28 with three non-bug tasks and no visible blockers. Inspected the repo shape and verified the actual stack is a Rust workspace plus Go verify-service tests, with `crates/setup-sql` still present in the workspace.
- Chose `.ralph/tasks/story-28-replace-setup-sql-with-docs-and-bash-scripts/01-task-remove-setup-sql-binary-and-all-references.md` and wrote it to `.ralph/current_task.txt` because removing the obsolete `setup-sql` crate and its references is the architectural prerequisite for the replacement docs and bash scripts. Next agent should execute that cleanup task, then validate `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint`.

- Read Ralph startup progress, confirmed the active task is story-28 task 01 removing the obsolete setup-sql binary and its references.
- Loaded the required tdd and improve-code-boundaries skill instructions so execution follows the mandated workflow.
- Next I am reading the task file and story directory to determine whether this turn must create/adjust a plan or execute an existing NOW EXECUTE plan.

- Read the active story-28 task and confirmed it has no attached plan yet; this turn is therefore a planning handoff, not execution.
- Inspected existing Ralph task/plan conventions and confirmed the task file should carry a <plan> entry while the plan file ends with a plan path plus NOW EXECUTE or TO BE VERIFIED.
- Next I am reading one representative plan so I can create the new story-28 plan in the expected format and stop immediately afterward.

- Mapped the real setup-sql reference surface across workspace metadata, workflows, runner test support, docs, README, and the setup-sql crate itself.
- Created and linked the execution plan at `.ralph/tasks/story-28-replace-setup-sql-with-docs-and-bash-scripts/01-task-remove-setup-sql-binary-and-all-references_plans/2026-04-27-remove-setup-sql-plan.md`.
- Marked the plan `NOW EXECUTE` because the deletion scope and boundary cleanup are now concrete: next turn should execute the four slices, run the required build/test/lint lanes, and only then complete the task.

- Read Ralph startup progress, current task path, task file, and the linked story-28 removal plan.
- Confirmed the plan is already marked NOW EXECUTE, so this turn must execute the setup-sql deletion plan rather than create a new plan.
- Read the required tdd and improve-code-boundaries skill instructions; next I am mapping all live setup-sql references and runner harness seams before editing.

- Deleted the `crates/setup-sql` crate and compose artifact, removed workspace and workflow wiring, and rewrote the README/docs around the remaining `runner` and `verify` surfaces.
- Flattened the runner test boundary that used to shell out to `setup-sql`: the E2E harnesses now build changefeed SQL directly from the test-owned mapping data and no longer keep `source_bootstrap` or `setup-sql` seams alive.
- Next I am regenerating `Cargo.lock`, then running the required build/test/lint lanes and fixing any compile drift before touching task metadata.
